title: Flawed Merge Sort on Doubly Linked List

statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.

hints.text: When merging two linked lists, be cautious about handling the `null` case for the next pointers of the nodes in the merged list.

editorial.title: Merge Sort on Doubly Linked List
commonMistakes: Incorrectly handling the `null` case for the next pointers of the nodes in the merged list

solutions.python:
// Python solution
from typing import List

class ListNode:
    def __init__(self, val=0, prev=None, next=None):
        self.val = val
        self.prev = prev
        self.next = next

class Solution:
    def sortList(self, head: ListNode):
        if not head or not head.next:
            return head

        mid = self.findMid(head)
        mid_next = mid.next
        mid.next = None

        self.mergeSort(head)
        self.mergeSort(mid_next)
        merged = self.merge(head, mid_next)

        return merged

    def merge(self, l: ListNode, r: ListNode) -> ListNode:
        if not l:
            return r
        if not r:
            return l
        if l.val <= r.val:
            l.next = self.merge(l.next, r)
            return l

        merged = ListNode(l.val)
        merged.next = self.merge(l.next, r)
        return merged

    def findMid(self, head: ListNode) -> ListNode:
        slow = fast = head
        while fast.next and fast.next.next:
            slow = slow.next
            fast = fast.next.next
        return slow

    def mergeSort(self, head: ListNode):
        # Recursively sort the sublists on either side of the midpoint
        self.sortList(head)
        self.sortList(head.next)

# Helper function to read input from sys.stdin and create a linked list
    def readList(self):
        line1, line2 = input().split('\n')
        n = int(line1)
        line3 = line2.split('\s')

        head = tail = ListNode(int(line3[0]))
        for num in line3[1:]:
            tail.next = ListNode(int(num))
            tail = tail.next

        return head

# Read input from sys.stdin and print output to sys.stdout
    def main(self):
        head = self.readList()
        sortedHead = self.sortList(head)

        # Print the sorted linked list to sys.stdout
        while sortedHead:
            print(sortedHead.val, end='\s')
            sortedHead = sortedHead.next
        print()

if __name__ == '__main__':
    s = Solution()
    s.main()
